MICROSOFT EDGE ADD-ONS LISTING
================================

Name: Ibid — Citation Manager

Short Description:
One-click citations in APA, MLA, Chicago, Harvard & 2,600+ styles. Privacy-first, Rust/WASM powered. No account needed.

Category: Productivity

Language: English

---

Detailed Description:

Ibid — from Latin "ibidem," meaning "in the same place" — is a citation manager built for students, researchers, and academics who need accurate references without compromising their privacy.

Ibid runs a full Citation Style Language (CSL) engine directly in your browser using WebAssembly. All citation processing happens locally on your device. No data is sent to external servers. No account is required.

HOW IT WORKS

1. Visit any webpage, journal article, or book page
2. Click the Ibid icon — metadata is extracted automatically
3. Choose your citation style and copy the formatted reference

Ibid supports one-click citing from academic publishers, news sites, and general webpages.

CITATION STYLES

74 styles bundled offline, including APA, MLA, Chicago, Harvard, and IEEE. 2,600+ additional styles from the official CSL repository can be downloaded on demand. Once downloaded, they work offline.

FEATURES

Citation & Formatting
  - Accurate bibliography and in-text citations
  - Parenthetical and narrative in-text toggle
  - Smart metadata extraction from multiple web standards (structured data, meta tags, and page content)
  - PDF metadata extraction via Rust/WASM — reads embedded text, document properties, and identifier patterns
  - Editable fields — review and correct any auto-detected data before saving
  - Auto-enhance — paste a DOI, ISBN, or other identifier to fill all fields from public academic APIs
  - Multi-source resolver with automatic fallback for reliable results
  - Title-based search fallback when no identifier is found

Library Management
  - Organize citations into projects
  - Star, tag, and search your references
  - Inline citation preview with per-entry style selection
  - Duplicate detection
  - Add entries manually with a built-in form
  - Side panel stays open while you research

Import & Export
  - Import and export in common academic formats (BibTeX, RIS, CSL-JSON, and more)
  - Filter exports by project, type, or starred status

Additional
  - Dark mode
  - Keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+Shift+C to cite)
  - Context menu integration
  - Session persistence — reopen the popup without losing your work

PRIVACY

  - All citation processing happens locally in your browser
  - No telemetry, analytics, or tracking
  - No data collection — your library stays on your device
  - Network requests only for identifier lookups and optional style downloads
  - Minimal required permissions — no broad host access
  - Optional scholarly API access — requested only when needed, you choose whether to grant it
  - Full privacy policy: https://ibid.tools/privacy

TECHNICAL DETAILS

Ibid uses a Rust/WebAssembly citation engine powered by Hayagriva (the CSL processor from the Typst project) for accurate rendering of all 2,600+ official CSL styles. An embedded English locale ensures correct terms ("et al.," month names, ordinals). The WASM engine also handles BibTeX and RIS parsing for fast import and export.

For questions, feedback, or bug reports:
  Website: https://ibid.tools
  GitHub: https://github.com/oriclabs/ibid
  Support: support@ibid.tools
  Privacy Policy: https://ibid.tools/privacy

---

NOTES FOR EDGE REVIEW

Edge uses the same Chromium extension format as Chrome. The package is identical to the Chrome Web Store submission.

Required permissions justification:
  - activeTab: Extract metadata from the current page when the user clicks the extension icon.
  - scripting: Inject the metadata extractor content script into the active tab on demand.
  - storage: Save the user's citation library, projects, and preferences locally.
  - sidePanel: Display the library management panel alongside the user's research.
  - contextMenus: Add citation options to the right-click menu.

Optional host permissions:
  - arxiv.org, export.arxiv.org: Fetch paper metadata from arXiv abstract pages.
  - api.semanticscholar.org: Fallback metadata for academic papers.
  - en.wikipedia.org: URL-based citation via Wikipedia's Citoid service.
  - doi.org: Follow DOI links to publisher pages for metadata extraction.

Optional permissions are never granted automatically. The user is prompted only when needed.

No broad permissions (<all_urls>, tabs, webRequest) are requested.

Content Security Policy includes wasm-unsafe-eval for WebAssembly execution only.
